what_it_does: This file is a minimal YAML-frontmatter attachment record that links a channel-memory entry in the design-source channel to an image asset located at image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-02.
observations: YAML frontmatter contains id att-d6904e3f, channel design-source, attachment_type images, target image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-02, and an added timestamp.
The file body is empty after the frontmatter.
The target uses a custom image:// URI scheme.
standouts: Attachment target is an image URI under recent-pitches/p-02 in the design-source channel.
Record is purely metadata with no embedded content.
concerns: The image:// target cannot be verified from this file alone.
There is no title, caption, or description for the attached image.
No author or provenance is recorded beyond the timestamp.
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points a design-source channel memory item to a pitch image asset.
questions: What visual content does p-02 contain?
Who added this attachment and why?
How is this attachment related to other channel-memory entries or attachments?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file records an image attachment for a design-source channel memory entry, which is exactly what its metadata fields describe.
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, retrievable association between a design-source channel memory entry and a specific pitch image so the image can be referenced as context.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme to address a pitch asset rather than a filesystem path or URL.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, id-stable link between a channel memory entry and an external image asset, allowing visual pitch context to be referenced without embedding binary data.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel-memory attachment record in the design-source channel pointing to the image asset image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-02.
intention_for_embedding: To associate a specific pitch image with a design-source channel memory entry for later retrieval and context.
